microenvironments: ME1 (early neurovascular microenvironment) -- with Schwann cells and endothelial cells
ME5 (late neurovascular microenvironment) -- with endothelial cells

functions.evidence: Co-expressed immunomodulatory genes including immune-inhibitory receptors (CX3CR1) and regulators of IL-6 production (SYT11). IL-6 downregulation confers anti-fibrogenic properties.
Gene module expression profiles suggested sprouting angiogenesis promoted by LYVE1+ and TML macrophages. RNAscope showed TML macrophages in close proximity to endothelial cells.
Co-located with Schwann cells in ME1. Expressed genes related to cell migration and neural development. Predicted to interact with Schwann cells contributing to synapse formation and axon guidance.
GAS6 expressed by TML macrophages predicted to interact with AXL receptors on WNT2+ fibroblasts, inducing immunosuppression and tissue repair.

key_evidence_quotes.quote: Prenatal skin TREM2+ microglia-like (TML) macrophages were highly correlated with embryonic brain microglia
key_evidence_quotes.section: Scarless healing and potential macrophage contribution

key_evidence_quotes.quote: sprouting angiogenesis (growth of new vessels) was promoted by LYVE1+ and TML macrophages
TML macrophages were also predicted to co-locate with Schwann cells in prenatal skin ('early neurovascular microenvironment', ME1)
This is in part contributed to by yolk-sac derived TML macrophages, which suggests that these cells have broader functions outside the central nervous system in early gestation

transcriptomic_correlation: TML macrophage had the highest proportion prediction to Mac4 (embryonic brain microglia) in logistic regression model trained on embryonic data from Suo et al. 2022
